titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Ronald Dworkin Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Eric Sonnenberg

Series IX, Computer Files, was processed and description added.

descriptivenote.p: The materials are predominantly in English. A small amount of material, primarily translations of Dworkin's work or materials relating to events Dworkin attended, is in Greek, Italian, Japanese, and Mandarin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Ronald Dworkin papers document Dworkin's life and career as a law professor, author, and legal philosopher. Materials primarily document his professional career and activities, including his work at Yale University, Oxford University, and New York University, as well as his academic and popular writing. A smaller amount of personal material is included, primarily from his youth.

The materials primarily contain writings and correspondence by Dworkin and other authors. Dworkin's writings include published and draft manuscript and electronic copies of books and articles, outlines, and notes. Writings by other authors include published copies, reprints, and photocopies of articles and books, and are often related to or discuss Dworkin's work. Correspondence includes typed letters in both physical and digital formats, as well as email. The collection also includes conference and event-related papers, course materials, personal financial and legal documents, research materials on specific subjects, and press clippings and reviews, along with a smaller amount of photographs, memorabilia, and ephemera. Many folders contain photocopies, clippings, or offprints of articles on a range of subjects. Groupings which focus on a specific theme have been noted. Many materials are annotated, primarily by Dworkin, but also by other unidentified authors. Dworkin's professional activities are also documented in digital and audiovisual material in a variety of formats.

Materials document Dworkin's activities from his youth through his final years. Coverage is inconsistent, however, and only a small amount of material exists for the period from the mid-1970s through the mid-1980s. The most well-documented time periods are the late 1960s and from the late 1980s until the 2010s.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Ronald Dworkin was a legal philosopher, law professor, and writer. He wrote widely in both the academic and popular press and was a faculty member at Yale University, Oxford University, New York University, and University College London. Dworkin was born on December 11, 1931 in Providence, Rhode Island. He earned a BA from Harvard University in 1953; a BA from Oxford University, where he was a Rhodes Scholar, in 1955; and an LLB from Harvard Law School in 1957.

After graduating, Dworkin clerked with Judge Learned Hand on the United States Second Circuit Court of Appeals. He then practiced law with the Sullivan & Cromwell firm in New York City. In 1962, he accepted a position as professor of law at Yale Law School, where he was later appointed to the Wesley N. Hohfeld Chair of Jurisprudence. From 1966 to 1969 he served as master of Trumbull College at Yale. In 1969, Dworkin left Yale to take a position at Oxford University, replacing H. L. A. Hart as chair of jurisprudence. Beginning in 1975, he held concurrent faculty positions at New York University and Oxford. At NYU, he founded and ran, with Thomas Nagel, the annual Colloquium in Legal, Political, and Social Philosophy. In 1997, Dworkin retired from Oxford and accepted a position as Professor of Jurisprudence at University College London.

Dworkin wrote and published extensively in both academic legal journals and the popular press. He published several books, including Taking Rights Seriously, Law's Empire, Sovereign Virtue, and Justice for Hedgehogs. In addition, he wrote frequently for a general audience in publications including the Observer (UK), the New York Times, and most frequently in the New York Review of Books, where he was a regular contributor from the late 1960s through 2013.
Dworkin married Betsy Ross in 1958; they had two children, Jennifer and Anthony. Betsy died in 2000, and Dworkin later married Irene Brendel. Dworkin died on February 14, 2013.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The papers are arranged in nine series: I. Numerical Files, 1948-2006. II. Conference and Event Files, 1968, 1987-2012. III. Correspondence, 1955-2012. IV. Course Materials, Circa 1963-2003. V. Personal Files, 1938-2013, undated. VI. Subject Files, 1987-2002, undated. VII. Writings, 1955-2013. VIII. Audiovisual Materials, Circa 1980s-circa 2000s. IX. Computer Files, Circa 1985-2013.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.

1TB WD My Passport Ultra HD external hard drive (Item 2017-m-0051-0194) in Series IX, Computer Files, is unprocessed and may contain sensitive information or be in a physical state that would prohibit use. Researchers wishing to request access should email mssa.assist@yale.edu. The request should outline the scope and purpose of the research project, why the researcher believes the material is relevant to their project, and contact information. If possible the request should also include a list of specific material of interest. The review may take several weeks.

Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy of non-commercially produced items for their personal use should consult the ordering reproductions information on the Manuscripts and Archives website.
Original computer files may not be accessed due to their fragility. Researchers must consult access copies.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright is retained by Irene Brendel Dworkin for materials authored or otherwise produced by Ronald Dworkin. After the lifetime of Irene Brendal Dworkin or until 1 May 2035, whichever comes first, copyright passes to Yale. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.
Copies of commercially produced audiovisual materials contained in this collection cannot be made for researcher use outside of the repository.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: The Ronald Dworkin papers were transferred to Yale in two portions. The first portion was transferred from London to the Yale Law Library and from the Yale Law Library to Manuscripts and Archives. The second portion was transferred from Dworkin's offices at New York University to Manuscripts and Archives.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Duplicate material and some financial and real estate records were removed from the collection and returned to the donor. Some original clippings and copies of Dworkin publications were photocopied for preservation, and the originals were returned to the donor. Commercially produced cassettes and videotapes were removed and returned to the donor. Books were transferred to the Lillian Goldman Law Library at Yale.

Approximately 331.4 gigabytes of computer files were removed from the digital materials in the collection, including deleted, duplicate, system, and software files. The laptops and hard drive backups contained accounts and documents from other users in addition to Dworkin. Materials from these other user accounts were identified when possible and removed from the collection.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Books were removed from the collection and transferred to the Lillian Goldman Law Library for individual cataloging.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Ronald Dworkin Papers (MS 2071). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Materials arrived to the repository in two accessions, one from London and one from New York City. These accessions were integrated during processing. All materials were refoldered and reboxed, and materials were rearranged to varying degrees.
More detailed notes on processing and arrangement are included in the description for each series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series comprises writings, press clippings and newspaper and magazine copies, correspondence, financial and legal documents, and other materials related to Ronald Dworkin's career as a writer, scholar, and professor. Some personal legal and financial documents are also included. These files consist of individual items and folders, all numbered and filed in numerical order by Dworkin. Items are generally marked with a number on the cover, first page, or folder, but numbers are not continuous, as many numbers are skipped or missing from the sequence

The writings in the numerical files primarily consist of offprints, photocopies, clippings, or printouts of published or draft versions of articles by other authors, as well as some writings by Dworkin. The writings often include a note or cover letter from the author. Many writings have annotations, primarily by Dworkin himself, but also by other unidentified authors. The authorship of many annotations is unclear. Materials with substantial annotations have been noted. The bulk of the writings are academic writings from the legal and philosophical fields, but they also include writings from popular newspapers and magazines.

The draft writings by Dworkin are often in a rough outline format and include drafts for articles as well as conference, course, and seminar lectures. Sometimes the final published title of the draft writing is unclear, or the draft is incomplete. Drafts have been identified when possible.

In addition to individual numbered items, the series contains a large number of numbered subject folders. These folders generally contain information on a topic or from a specific event. These may contain documents from across a wide range of time, regardless of their place in the numerical filing scheme. Documents within each subject folder are not generally numbered individually.

The series also contains a limited amount of correspondence. The bulk of the correspondence in the series is found in various subject folders; in general, however, the extent of correspondence is limited, and is primarily incoming correspondence. Correspondence outside the subject folders consists almost entirely of cover letters to articles or other writings.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials are filed in numerical order, as numbered and filed by Dworkin. The materials are divided into two groupings: "Arranged by Dworkin," consisting of materials filed in numerical order when received by Manuscripts and Archives, and "Unarranged," consisting of numbered materials which arrived mixed with other material, which were grouped and arranged during processing. The order of the files is roughly chronological, and generally proceeds from circa 1977 through circa 2011. However, the order is very approximate, and many exceptions occur. In general, it appears that materials are filed roughly chronologically by the time during which Dworkin used the item or folder, rather than by their date of creation.

otherfindaid.head: Other Finding Aids
otherfindaid.p: A partial inventory of the series, created by Dworkin, is located in Series I.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Most materials were filed in numerical order by Dworkin when received by Manuscripts and Archives and are arranged under the "Arranged by Dworkin" heading. Occasional misfilings in this group were corrected during processing.

A smaller amount of numbered materials arrived mixed with other, unnumbered material. This numbered material was rearranged in numerical order during processing and is listed under the "Unarranged" heading.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Article offprints, by various authors, some with annotations by Dworkin or notes from the author. Topics include legal gaps and the "no right answer" thesis.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Loose typescript writings, possibly including portions of drafts for unidentified articles or lectures. Some have handwritten notes and edits by Dworkin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopies and offprints of articles by various authors. Documents include " Legislation on Human Rights ", a 1976 "discussion document" published by a United Kingdom government working group studying a proposed bill of rights.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clippings and photocopies of reviews of Taking Rights Seriously .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Offprints and photocopies of articles and papers, some with author notes or annotations by an unidentified author. Topics vary, and several respond directly to Dworkin arguments.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopied articles, by various authors, on economic equity and economic fairness.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopies and offprints of articles and reviews, by various authors, including Dworkin. Some include author notes. Many respond to Dworkin's writings, especially Taking Rights Seriously .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopies and offprints of articles and reviews, by various authors, including Dworkin. Some include annotations by Dworkin or author notes. Many respond to Dworkin's writings, especially Taking Rights Seriously .

Includes an issue of the New York Review of Books (January 20, 1983) with an article by Dworkin; an issue of the London Review of Books (September 3-16, 1981); and a photocopied booklet of biographical notes and tributes to Gareth Evans.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopies and offprints of articles and papers, by various authors, some with author cover letters or annotations by an unidentified author.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.p: Photocopied notes by John Rawls for a series of eight lectures on Immanuel Kant.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Article offprints, by various authors including Dworkin, some with author inscriptions to Dworkin. Also includes an undated draft printout of a letter to Amartya Sen, responding to Sen's points in a recent seminar on equality; and a transcript, with cover letter, of a Dworkin lecture at a symposium on "Civil Disobedience" in Bonn, Germany. Note that a Tablet article about the Bonn Civil Disobedience conference can be found in numerical file #1303.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials related to the publication of Dworkin's response to a previously unpublished essay by John Mackie, titled " Bootstraps Enterprises ", in a memorial volume after Mackie's death. Includes correspondence from Mackie's widow Joan Mackie, photocopies of Mackie's article, and many pages of unidentified draft writings, probably by Dworkin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopies and offprints of articles and papers, by various authors, some with annotations by Dworkin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopies and offprints of articles and papers, by various authors, some with author cover notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printouts of draft writings on equality by Dworkin, with annotations by Dworkin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopies and offprints of articles and papers, by various authors including Dworkin, some with annotations by Dworkin. Includes printout drafts of three Dworkin writings titled " The Value In Life ", " Rights as Trumps ", and " What is Jurisprudence About ".

Also includes a small amount of correspondence, primarily from Dworkin's Oxford University colleagues, and a transcript of a 1972 appearance by Dworkin on the BBC TV program Ad Lib .

Also includes newspaper and journal clippings of articles by and about Dworkin (mostly from the early 1970s), and an issue of Not The New York Times , a satirical newspaper published during the New York City printers' strike of 1978.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printouts of draft writings, outlines, and notes, some with annotations by Dworkin. Topics include jurisprudential theory, the "new right", and agent relativity.

Hart, noted in inventory as "comments for a seminar at Oxford"; and photocopies, offprints, and typed copies of articles and papers, by various authors. Also contains correspondence regarding a dispute with an Oxford University student about Dworkin's supervision. Note that additional material regarding this dispute can be found in numerical file #1347.

Also includes outline notes, likely by Dworkin, for unidentified lectures or articles on equality, and a transcript of speaking remarks by Dworkin on academic freedom.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, plans, invoices, and legal and financial records related to the purchase of Dworkin's home on Martha's Vineyard, and later construction and repair work done on the property.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reports and correspondence, with annotations by Dworkin and unidentified authors, on the proposed merger of the Bedford College (London) philosophy department with the department at King's College, University of London.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopies, offprints, and clippings of articles and papers by various authors, including Dworkin, some with cover notes or letters. Also includes handwritten lecture notes on Robert Nozick and press clippings and photocopies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

Also includes typescript lecture notes for a seminar on liberalism (Michaelmas Term 1984), and press clippings regarding a 1984 Dworkin speech at the University of Virginia Law School.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

Also includes articles, lecture outlines, and correspondence from G. A. Cohen regarding his presentations for a 1985 seminar on "Freedom and Consequence," with annotations by Dworkin.

Also includes publisher correspondence about Law's Empire and A Matter of Principle , and a book jacket proof for Law's Empire , with a cover letter.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopies and clippings of Dworkin interviews and book reviews for Taking Rights Seriously , Matter of Principles , and Law's Empire .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopies and offprints of articles by various authors; correspondence containing comments from legal scholars about Dworkin's 1990 Tanner Lecture; and manuscript notes by Dworkin for his Mishcon de Reya lecture on "Religion, Speech, Conscience".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, schedules, readings, notes, and other materials concerning a colloquium on "The Expansion of the Liberal Society", sponsored by the Institut für die Wissenschaften vom Menschen in Vienna, August 20-22, 1992.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopies, offprints, and clippings of articles by various authors, including Dworkin, some with cover notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various readings related to Dworkin's trip to Prague in May 1992. Readings include articles and the text of proposed resolutions in the Czechoslovakian parliament.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopies of articles and papers by various authors. Also includes reader correspondence to the New York Review of Books editorial staff regarding a 1991 Dworkin article on free speech and a clipping of 1991 Frankfurter Allgemeine article on Dworkin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, lecture transcript and draft notes, travel documents and receipts, and other materials relating to Dworkin's participation in a colloquium titled "The French Look at America", organized by New York University in Paris in June 1991.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Draft of an article by Ruth Chang, with annotations by Dworkin; correspondence between Dworkin and John Finnis, 1998, regarding indeterminacy and incommensurability; a French translation of a Dworkin speech in Louvain; and a draft of an "article for Italian paper" titled " The Left, Equality, and the Good Life ".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Faxed copies of letters to the editor regarding Dworkin's 1992 article in the New York Review of Books on free speech and Anthony Lewis's book Make No Law .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Steven Knapp about interpretation; photocopies and offprints of articles by various authors, including Dworkin, some with cover notes; and a booklet of speeches given at Eva Colorni's funeral.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, papers, lecture notes and outlines, business cards, travel documents, schedules, and other materials related to Dworkin's travel to and lectures in Japan in 1991.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Issue of New York Review of Books with a Dworkin article titled " The Press on Trial ".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Draft and publisher correspondence for a Dworkin article titled " Pragmatism, Right Answers, and True Banality ", with annotations by Dworkin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopies and offprints of articles and papers by various authors; an early draft outline of Life's Dominion , 1991; and correspondence and a draft reply to a letter to the editor discussing a Dworkin article in the New York Review of Books on the sanctity of life.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, legal documents, and other materials relating to a lawsuit brought against Dworkin by a London neighbor regarding construction work on Dworkin's property.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, a conference program, articles, lecture notes and outlines, photographs, and other materials related to Dworkin's participation in a conference on "Identity and Diversity in a Democratic Europe," held in Brussels in May 1991.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopies, clippings, and offprints of articles by various authors, including Dworkin, some with cover notes; correspondence, schedules, and other materials from a July 1991 conference on "Ethics and Economics" at the University of Siena; a draft of a Dworkin article for Pouvoirs titled " Constitutional Controversy ," with publisher correspondence; and notes for a Dworkin talk on free speech, spring 1992.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Transcript of a 1991 lecture on liberalism given by Dworkin in Florence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript draft of " Understanding Dworkin " by John C. Vlahoplus

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopies, drafts, and offprints of articles and papers, by various authors, including Dworkin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Offprints of articles by various authors including Dworkin; Catholic pamphlets on abortion and euthanasia, some with annotations, probably by Dworkin; and a Dworkin speech to film classifiers, titled " Human Rights and Freedom of Expression - A Constitutional View ".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts and offprints of articles by various authors. Also includes a schedule for a conference in Barcelona in November 1992.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A European Court of Human Rights opinion on abortion, and lecture draft notes and outlines for Oxford University seminars, Michaelmas Term 1992.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence from readers and publishers, and press clippings, related to Dworkin's "Counterblast" article in the Observer on a Bill of Rights for Britain.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, financial documents, rental agreements, repair documents, and related correspondence regarding Dworkin's homes in London, New York, and Martha's Vineyard. Materials primarily relate to remodeling, repairs, and purchases for the properties.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence with Albie Sachs, with lecture transcripts, articles, and legislative drafts, regarding the drafting of the South African constitution and bill of rights.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Draft outlines for three Dworkin lectures or articles, with annotations by Dworkin: a speech for H. L. A. Hart's memorial service, "Reply to Ricoeur", and "Outline of Further Thoughts about Interpretation." Also includes a press clipping on a proposed obscenity law in Maine.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopies and offprints of articles by various authors, some with author cover notes. Also includes a schedule for a conference on "Consensus and Democracy: A Debate around John Rawls" held at Oxford University in February 1993.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopies and offprints of articles by various authors, including Dworkin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscript for Dworkin's McGill Lecture in Jurisprudence and Public Policy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopies and offprints of articles by various authors, including Dworkin, some with cover notes. Also includes a cover letter and three short draft pieces by Dworkin: "Law and Morals," "Indeterminacy in Law," and "Internal and External Skepticism."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopies of articles by various authors, including Dworkin, and a transcript of Dworkin's replies to other speakers at a Naples event on "Diritto e moralita" (Law and Morality), 1992.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

L. A. Hart's memorial ceremony, 1993.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series includes schedules, notes, correspondence, and other materials related to conferences, lectures, trips, and other events attended or organized by Dworkin.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials are arranged chronologically by the date of the event.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Files were generally arranged together by Dworkin. In a few cases, materials from the same events were grouped together during processing.

1996 Order of the Coif lecture at Arizona State University
China and Hong Kong trip correspondence and related material
American Jewish Historical Society lecture correspondence, research, and related material
Balzan Prize lecture drafts, correspondence, and related material
Conference and event flyers, schedules, and pamphlets

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains Dworkin's professional correspondence. Correspondence with prominent individuals and organizations in academia and law are filed in individual folders, as are those of correspondents with whom Dworkin exchanged three or more letters. Groupings of correspondence created by Dworkin on a specific topic have been retained. All other correspondence is grouped in general folders by letter and arranged in alphabetical order. Additional correspondence can be found in other series, particularly the Numerical Files, Writings, and Computer Files series. Some early Dworkin family correspondence is filed in the Personal Files series.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials are arranged alphabetically by correspondent and topic.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Correspondence was filed by Dworkin both chronologically and loosely with other unsorted materials. Correspondence was separated from unsorted materials and re-sorted by correspondent during processing.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

processinfo.head: Processing Information

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains photographs, awards, and ephemera documenting personal and family activities from Dworkin's childhood and college years. It includes correspondence received by Dworkin's mother, Madeline Talamo. Additional personal correspondence, notes, and photographs can be found in the Computer Files series.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials are grouped into two groupings: Family and childhood materials and Awards, photographs, and ephemera. Materials are arranged alphabetically within each grouping.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Materials were originally filed loosely with materials from other series and were gathered thematically during processing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains published and typescript and manuscript draft writings and lectures by Dworkin, as well as writings by other authors. The series also includes photocopies and offprints of published materials. Some correspondence and research materials pertaining to the writings are filed with the writings. Note that additional writings, both drafts and published writings, can be found in other series, particularly the Numerical Files, Subject Files, and Computer Files series.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials written by Dworkin are grouped into four groupings: Article files, Book files, Unidentified short writings, notes, and drafts, and Publisher and publicity materials. Article files and Book files are arranged alphabetically by title, and Unidentified short writings, notes, and drafts and Publisher and publicity materials are arranged chronologically.

Materials by other authors are grouped into Drafts, Notes and short writings, and Printed material. Drafts are arranged alphabetically by the author's last name; Notes and short writings and Printed material are arranged alphabetically by folder title.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Materials were arranged by Dworkin into article files, as well as loosely filed with other materials. Writings were collocated and arranged during processing. Materials not clearly identifiable with an article, book, or other title were arranged in the Unidentified short writings, notes, and drafts and Drafts groupings. Notepad pages were detatched from notepad backings and foldered individually during processing.

Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy of non-commercially produced items for their personal use should consult the ordering reproductions information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of computer files documenting Ronald Dworkin's life and professional career as a law professor, legal scholar, and author. Materials include writings by Dworkin and other authors; correspondence, including typed letters and emails; course and teaching materials; and audiovisual materials.

Writings sometimes duplicate published articles or materials available in physical form in other series. However, a significant amount of unique writings are included, along with draft materials in a variety of stages. Correspondence in this series is significantly more extensive than that found elsewhere in the papers, and generally does not duplicate other materials. It includes both typed letters in digital format and emails, and covers a wide range of personal and professional correspondence which primarily documents Dworkin's professional relationships and activities as a scholar, professor, and author.

Files were extracted from one laptop, two external hard drives, two CD-Rs, two VCDs, eighty-four 3.5" floppy disks, and one 5.25" floppy disk. Materials created by Dworkin are primarily in common word processing, audiovisual, and email archive file formats. Particularly common formats include Microsoft Office documents in .doc, .docx, .xls, and .xlsx formats; documents in PDF, Wordperfect, and XYWrite formats; and email archives and backups in .pst, .emlx, .olk14Message, and .olk14MsgSource formats.

Floppy disks were used in Dworkin's office at New York University and document his teaching and courses there, along with his writings for the New York Review of Books and other publications. The hard drives, laptop, and CD-Rs contain writings by Dworkin and other authors, course materials, correspondence, and other personal and professional materials.

Digital materials span circa 1985 to 2013 and are especially extensive for roughly 2005-2013. Dates listed in this finding aid were generally determined by file creation or modification dates. However, dates in the metadata for some files reflect dates of backups or transfers by the creator or donor. In these cases, processing staff provided approximate dates for this finding aid based on a survey of the files.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: 1TB WD My Passport Ultra HD external hard drive (Item 2017-m-0051-0194) in Series IX, Computer Files, is unprocessed and may contain sensitive information or be in a physical state that would prohibit use. Researchers wishing to request access should contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu. The request should outline the scope and purpose of the research project, why the researcher believes the material is relevant to their project, and contact information. If possible the request should also include a list of specific material of interest. The review may take several weeks.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series IX is arranged into four groupings: external hard drives, laptop, optical media, and floppy disks. Items are arranged chronologically within each grouping.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: After accessioning materials through the Yale University Digital Accessioning Support Service (DASS) in 2018, Manuscripts and Archives staff reviewed and processed materials using Forensic Toolkit (FTK) software. Using FTK, several categories of files were identified and removed: deleted files, duplicate files, file slack, files in the Recycle Bin, Thumbs.db files, resource fork files, DS_Store files, and Quicken QDATA files. Files were checked against the NSRL Reference Data Set to identify common software and operating system files, and these were also removed. Some disks and hard drive user accounts were created by users other than Ronald Dworkin. Materials from these other user accounts were identified when possible and removed from the collection. For all retained digital materials, the original disks have been retained as well.

Processing staff attempted to maintain file and folder metadata wherever possible. However, due to technical limitations, folder, directory, and file metadata was changed or added in some cases, and reflects the date of processing in 2021. Individual file metadata has been maintained and reflects the state of the files upon their transfer to Manuscripts and Archives. Original metadata for all directories, folders, and files has been retained in file manifests generated during processing.
